page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, yielding an 8 GB RAM per vCPU ratio. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor based on the x86_64 architecture, the instance allocates 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU configuration includes a 32 MB L3 cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache, with hardware support for AVX, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI cryptographic instructions. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded operations, such as bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core tests, fall into the lower performance tiers. Memory read bandwidth is relatively weak, though write bandwidth for large blocks and single-threaded compression tasks maintain average performance levels.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the n2d-highmem-4 provides a high memory-to-vCPU density without the overhead of high-core-count licensing. It is suited for workloads that require a larger memory footprint but do not demand intensive parallel processing, such as caching layers, lightweight databases, and memory-bound single-threaded applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine configured with 4 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, utilizing an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 2 physical cores with hyper-threading, a 32 MB L3 cache, and dedicated CPU allocation. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU execution, though multi-core and memory read bandwidth benchmarks are positioned in lower percentiles. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core makes this instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. It is best suited for lightweight databases, caching systems, and memory-bound single-threaded applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and strong single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.
